species: Cougar and Gray Wolf -- territorial overlap encounter
sound_type: Territorial overlap encounter: Cougar warning vocalization (low-frequency growl escalating to scream; 80-4,000 Hz) plus Gray Wolf response (staccato bark-growl-howl sequence; 150-1,200 Hz fundamental); dual-apex-predator boundary acoustic event; rare in practice; documented in areas of wolf recolonization into established Cougar range
ecosystem: Transitional forest-open interface -- where dense conifer forest meets river corridor or meadow opening; both species use these transition zones as travel corridors; encounters highest probability at riparian edges and ridge saddles shared by both species
season: Year-round potential; highest encounter probability during Wolf pack movement in late winter-early spring (territorial expansion) and during prey concentration events (deer/elk winter concentration areas)
conservation_status: Cougar: Least Concern (IUCN); stable in PNW. Gray Wolf: Least Concern (IUCN globally); recovering in Washington State (recolonization from Idaho, ongoing). Dual-apex-predator overlap is a marker of recovering ecosystem health.
milestone: FIRST DUAL-APEX ENCOUNTER IN ENGINE. E=9. First entry documenting acoustic event between two apex predators at territorial boundary. Cougar-Wolf overlap is a marker of successful wolf recolonization into historic PNW range. POST-SUMMIT FIRST DEGREE: E=9 sustained at degree 313, continuing from the Summit maximum zone (308-312).

coordinates.description: Northeast Washington State (Okanogan Highlands, Kettle Range) and Cascade Range east slope: primary zones of documented Cougar-Wolf co-occurrence in Washington State. Wolf packs (Smackout, Togo, Wedge, Sherman packs) overlap with established Cougar territories. Documented encounters increasing as wolf population grows.

acoustic_data.vocalization_type: Compound event: Cougar warning vocalization (low growl to ascending scream sequence) plus Wolf response (staccato bark-growl-howl). Neither species produces this acoustic combination in isolation; the encounter produces the dual-apex compound acoustic event.
acoustic_data.mechanics: Cougar warning: begins as low-frequency chest growl (80-200 Hz), escalates to mid-range snarl (400-1,500 Hz), can culminate in the Cougar scream (800-4,000 Hz) at peak threat. Wolf response: staccato warning bark (150-600 Hz fundamental), transitions to growl-bark combination, may escalate to group howl if pack-present. The sequencing is asymmetric: Cougar typically initiates; Wolf pack responds collectively.
acoustic_data.frequency_range: Combined event: Cougar 80-4,000 Hz; Wolf 150-1,200 Hz fundamental (harmonics to 3,000 Hz). Overlap zone 150-1,200 Hz where both animals are producing simultaneously. The combined frequency space is broader than either animal alone.
acoustic_data.duration: Encounter duration: seconds to minutes depending on outcome. The acoustic event itself (initial contact to disengagement): typically 30 seconds to 3 minutes. Brief by duration; extreme by acoustic intensity.
acoustic_data.e9_calibration: E=9: Five factors: (1) two apex predators simultaneously vocalizing at threat level (additive acoustic intensity), (2) rarity -- dual-apex encounters are uncommon; each event carries ecological weight as a documented inter-predator boundary event, (3) frequency breadth of combined event (80-4,000 Hz spans near-infrasound to upper-mid range), (4) behavioral context (territorial boundary at maximum threat), (5) ecosystem significance -- Wolf-Cougar encounter marks recovery of dual-apex ecology in PNW, a decades-long conservation milestone made audible. Combined: E=9.

nodes.title: First Dual-Apex Predator Encounter in Engine
nodes.description: The Cougar-Wolf territorial encounter is the first SPS entry documenting acoustic interaction between two apex predators. Previous E=9 entries (Marten 308, Great Horned Owl pair 309, Orca 311) involved single-species events. Degree 313 is the first multi-species apex encounter: both animals vocalizing at territorial maximum simultaneously.

nodes.title: Wolf Recolonization as Ecosystem Signal
nodes.description: Gray Wolf recolonization of Washington State (beginning early 2000s from Idaho dispersal) made Cougar-Wolf encounters possible where they had been absent for decades. The acoustic event documented here is only possible because wolves returned. The encounter is a signal of ecosystem recovery: dual-apex presence is a high-trophic-complexity marker.

nodes.title: Post-Summit E=9 Continuation -- Degree 313
nodes.description: The Grunge Summit SPS maximum zone (308-312) ended at degree 312. Degree 313 continues at E=9. The Cougar-Wolf encounter sustains the SPS maximum in the post-Summit zone. The SPS track does not decline when the Summit ends; it maintains maximum energy through a new acoustic category: dual-apex encounter.

nodes.title: Territorial Boundary as Acoustic Generator
nodes.description: The encounter's acoustic intensity is maximized because both animals are at a territorial boundary -- the disputed edge of their respective ranges. Away from the boundary, neither produces threat vocalizations at this intensity. The boundary itself is the acoustic generator: the space where territories contest is where sound is produced at maximum.

nodes.title: Alice in Chains -- Cougar/Wolf Equivalence
nodes.description: AIC's dual vocal (Cantrell-Staley: two voices at contested harmonic boundaries, dissonant intervals held without resolution) paired with the Cougar-Wolf encounter (two apex animals at contested territorial boundary, both vocalizing simultaneously). Both are boundary events. Both produce sound at the edge of collision. Both E=9.

cross_references.relationship: E=9 + E=9 = E=18. POST-SUMMIT FIRST DEGREE. AIC dual vocal (two voices at contested harmonic boundary) + Cougar-Wolf encounter (two apex animals at contested territorial boundary). Boundary events. Both E=9.

Preceding SPS E=9 entry (Summit final degree). Storm surge was meteorological E=9; Cougar-Wolf encounter is biological E=9. Post-Summit E=9 continuation. Different acoustic category, same maximum energy.

Grizzly was the engine's first single-apex-predator E=8 entry. Cougar-Wolf is the first dual-apex encounter at E=9. The predator-sequence in the SPS engine: single apex (304, E=8) -> maximum single (308, Marten E=9) -> dual apex (313, E=9).
The Gray Wolf in isolation (solo howl, pack chorus) will enter the SPS engine in future degrees. The 313 encounter establishes the Wolf in the engine at dual-apex context before its solo entry.
